2x = 24

subject: Mathematics
question_text:
5. The centre of the circle ABC is O. If its radius is 8cm and < ACB = 40o, Calculate the
length of the
(a) Chord AB
(b) Perpendicular OM
Length of the chord AB = 2/MB/ = 2 x 5.142
= 10.28cm = 10.3cm approx
(b) /OM/= 8 Cos 40o or 8Sin50o
=6.128cm = 6.13cm = 6.1cm
ALITER: Using Pythagoras’ rule
OM = √ (OB) 2 – (MB) 2 = 6.1cm
marking_scheme:
< AOB = 2<ABC = 2 x 40o = 80o
< BOM = ½ of 80o = 40o
Considering triangle OMB,
/MB/ = 8 sin 40o
or 8 Cos 50O = 5.142cm

subject: Mathematics
question_text:
6(a) Find the value of a and b in the figure below
(b) Five years ago, a father was twice as old as his son. In 4 years’ time, the sum of their
ages will be 78. Find their present ages.
Solution(a) b = 180 o – 120 o = 60 o (opposite angles in cyclic quad are supplementary)
Considering ∆ACD,
a + b + 80 o =180 o (s in a ∆)
 a = 180 o – 80 o – 60 o = 40 o
(b) Let the present ages be son, x yrs, father y yrs,
then 5 years ago, we have
y – 5 = 2(x-5)
 2x – y = 5 ___________ (1)
in 4 years’ time, we have
(x+ 4) + (y+4) = 78
 x+y = 70 ____________ (2)
From (1) and (2), we have, x = 25 and y = 45
 their present ages are son = 25 yrs, father = 45 years
(a) ALITER
5 year ago if son is y year’s old father was 2y years old. In 4 years time, son will be
(y+5+4) yrs
father = (2y+5+4) yrs
which gives y + 9 + 2y + 8 = 78; y = 20
the present ages are y + 5 = 25yrs and 2y + 5 = 45yrs for the son and father respectively.

n2 n

5x = 500 o

= ₦1,525.00
